title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for source registration, subscriptions, following, and ownership and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for shared-source and tenant subscription model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Source Registration · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Source Registrati…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 2107 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Concurrent Registration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 3237 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use unique constraints and transaction retry to reconcile simultaneous equivalent source registrations.
